blocks.text: found three things about the sky he would have missed otherwise. He counted that as a win.
looked at the water. He was still processing, but quietly. That was as much as he could promise.
caught him looking once. She looked back at the water without saying anything.
sat with the water for a while. The fire eventually got actually low and people started heading in.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Good night, you two. Come find us on the beach tomorrow — we'll be out there most of the day. No plans. Just show up.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Thank you for having us. Seriously — this was a really good night.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: We'll do it again tomorrow. Come back hungry.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: That was actually really good. I wasn't sure — new people, first night, you always hope it isn't going to be awkward. But that wasn't awkward at all.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Parts that weren't you running behavioral profiles on people trying to enjoy a bonfire. What you think those parts?

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Good. And the Appalachian Trail story.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: That you're capable of it. In case you try to tell me later that nothing happened on this vacation and it was just three days.
She said that like she believed more than three days were possible. I didn't argue.
She said it differently. Like the night had meant something and she was naming the end of it. I didn't know what to do with that.

blocks.prompt: She said it differently. What does he do with that?

options.label: He pauses at the door. One beat. Then goes inside.
He looks back. She's still standing there, facing the water.
